arts.ttl: Stoke City team news vs QPR as Mark Robins makes three changes
arts.sum: It’s games of football when we’ve got to pick points up, which we’ve been close to doing. Sam Gallagher starts his first game in nearly three months as Mark Robins makes three changes to his Stoke City starting line-up for a home game against Queens Park Rangers.

arts.ttl: Stoke v QPR line-ups: No Frey, Varane and Morgan return for Rangers
arts.sum: QPR will play without a recognised striker this afternoon as Michael Frey did not travel with the squad. The fit-again Karamoko Dembele starts, with Lucas Andersen and Paul Smyth supporting him in attack. With Steve Cook out injured, Morgan Fox partners Ronnie Edwards at the back. Jonathan Varane returns in midfield, as does Kieran Morgan. […] The post Stoke v QPR line-ups: No Frey, Varane and Morgan return for Rangers appeared first on West London Sport.

arts.ttl: Stoke City vs QPR live - Team news from the bet365 Stadium - Stoke-on-Trent Live
arts.sum: It is tight down at the bottom and the Potters went into this weekend just one point above the dotted line - but there are at least eight clubs who are panicking and Mark Robins would ideally like to drag a couple more into the mix over the next few weeks. We will have all the news, action and reaction as it happens.

arts.ttl: Boss buoyed by 'biggest turnaround' in his time at Stoke City despite 'huge blow'
arts.sum: Stoke fired eight blanks in 17 league games between late November and mid-February and Robins, who took charge on New Year’s Day, wasn’t helped that 11-goal top scorer Tom Cannon was soon whisked back to Leicester to be sold on to Sheffield United. Mark Robins believes that getting Stoke City even looking like they can score goals has been one of the most important turnarounds in his first three months as manager.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins makes emphatic point on referees as Stoke City move on
arts.sum: He said: “There are obviously reasons for things but we’ve got to help ourselves too. Decisions have gone against us, there’s no two ways about it, but there’s no point dwelling on any of that because it doesn’t help.

arts.ttl: New Stoke City head of recruitment has a 10-year problem to fix
arts.sum: Beyond those players we’ve managed to get stuck in a short-term cycle of loans and disposable signings that haven’t worked. It feels like if Sol Sidibe was at a Premier League club – and plenty have wanted him over the years – we’d probably try to sign him on loan.

arts.ttl: QPR dealt injury blow ahead of Stoke City trip
arts.sum: Jonathan Varane (hamstring) and Alfie Lloyd (ankle) have both joined in training this week, however, and could be back involved in a match that could end with the gap between these two teams as narrow as three points or as big as nine. The 33-year-old centre-back has been managing a foot injury he picked up in December and limped out of a 2-2 draw with Leeds United heading into the international break.

arts.ttl: Predicted Stoke City starting XI vs QPR as Mark Robins chases big home win
arts.sum: Mark Robins will be hammering home to his Stoke City players what they have to do, beyond winning, to make sure they can ride a wave of home support. We’ll need that to continue and players understand what supporters want to see from them.

arts.ttl: QPR captain Cook to miss Stoke game
arts.sum: Steve Cook has been ruled out of QPR’s trip to Stoke. The captain has been struggling with a foot injury he initially suffered against Preston in December. Cook, who turns 34 next month, went off during the 2-2 draw with Leeds prior to the international break and has not been able to train fully since. arts.ttl: Derby County sign Stoke City old boy in injury emergency
arts.sum: But then the gaffer (Eustace) sent me a text asking, ‘What's your situation?’ Obviously Derby is a bit closer to home than Middlesbrough, so I was like, ‘I'm still a free agent and I'm ready to go’. The defender - who has played at centre-half as well as left-back since leaving Stoke in 2019 - has been training with the squad over the last couple of weeks since a short trial at Middlesbrough.
